text.title: Token discipline for the orchestrator
text.summary: The orchestrator is the most expensive context. Five hard rules keep it lean: file-reference dispatch, on-disk subagent outputs, bounded summaries, default background mode for long work, and no narration of internal deliberation.

rule.statement: Every subagent dispatch MUST pass two file paths — master-prompt + task brief — and nothing else beyond a small parameter block. The subagent reads the prompts from disk itself.
rule.rationale: Pasting the full prompt into the dispatch message duplicates content the subagent will read anyway, inflates the orchestrator's input tokens, and makes per-task brief edits invisible to future runs.

example.code:
You are <Phase-X> subagent for project <slug>.
Read these files first, in order: 1. /<abs path>/.product/master-prompt.md 2. /<abs path>/.product/tasks/<NN-task>.md
Execute the task as specified. Save full output to <output-path> BEFORE returning. Return summary under <N> words.
example.note: Two paths plus a word cap. The task brief carries the rest.

antipattern.description: Inline-pasting a 1500-word prompt body into the dispatch message instead of pointing at the task brief on disk.
antipattern.reason: The orchestrator pays for those tokens on every dispatch and on every summary that quotes the prompt. The brief on disk is paid for once.

rule.statement: Subagents MUST save full output to `research/NN-<slug>.md` (or feature SDD files for Phase 3) BEFORE returning a summary. Summaries are bounded to 300-400 words unless the task brief says otherwise.
rule.rationale: The full output is the artifact; the summary is a pointer. The orchestrator never re-reads the full output unless an architectural decision depends on a specific quote — and even then, a sub-subagent reads it, not the orchestrator.

rule.statement: Long-running research and heavy computation MUST run with `run_in_background: true`. The orchestrator continues with other work and is notified on completion.
rule.rationale: Foreground-mode subagents block the orchestrator and waste cron ticks waiting. Background mode lets parallel research dimensions advance simultaneously and lets the orchestrator handle other phase logic in the same tick.

section.title: Orchestrator does not redo subagent work

rule.statement: If a question can be answered by re-reading a research file, the orchestrator MUST dispatch a sub-subagent (or trust the existing summary) instead of reading the file itself.
rule.rationale: Re-reading the full research output puts those tokens back into the orchestrator's context. Sub-subagent dispatch keeps the cost in a disposable context that ends when the answer arrives.

rule.statement: Status updates from the orchestrator are emitted at decision points only — phase transitions, subagent dispatch, irreversible-action gate, halt. Internal reasoning is not narrated.
rule.rationale: Narration is read by the operator and consumes tokens for content the next tick will not need. Decision points are the natural cut where the operator wants visibility; everything else is overhead.
